Title: Social Psychology Quarterly
Description: Core journal for sociological social psychology. It publishes theoretical and empirical work on the link between individuals, groups, institutions, and social structure.
Tags: journal; sociological social psychology; interaction; self and society; social structure
Notes: Essential reference point; link to journal page rather than hosting PDFs.

Title: Handbook of Social Psychology
Author / Institution: John DeLamater & Amanda Ward, eds. / Springer
Description: Broad handbook covering major theoretical perspectives and topics in social psychology, including interactionist, identity, social exchange, and social structure approaches.
Tags: handbook; theory; identity; interactionism; social exchange; social structure
Notes: Recommended as a reference link, not as a hosted file.

Title: Handbook of the Social Psychology of Inequality
Author / Institution: Jane D. McLeod, Edward J. Lawler & Michael Schwalbe, eds. / Springer
Description: Major reference on how social psychological processes mediate, reproduce, and contest inequality. Strong fit for RC42 themes on status, power, identity, stigma, and justice.
Tags: inequality; status; power; stigma; justice; intersectionality; handbook
Notes: Strong candidate for the first publication list.

Title: Contemporary Social Psychological Theories
Author / Institution: Peter J. Burke, ed. / Stanford University Press
Description: Presents major theory programs in sociological social psychology, including identity, exchange, affect control, expectation states, and related approaches.
Tags: theory; identity theory; exchange theory; affect control theory; expectation states; symbolic interactionism
Notes: Useful for theory-oriented teaching and reading lists.

Title: Social Identity: Sociological and Social Psychological Perspectives
Description: Classic special issue connecting social identity theory with sociological and social psychological perspectives. Useful for identity, group processes, and boundary-making themes.
Tags: social identity; identity; group processes; special issue; classic
Notes: Classic resource; check access conditions before presenting as open.

Title: Emotions and Society
Description: Interdisciplinary journal focused on emotions as social, cultural, political, and relational phenomena. Relevant for sociology of emotions and affective dimensions of social life.
Tags: emotions; affect; culture; politics; journal
Notes: Could be linked as a journal resource; selected articles may be open access.

Title: On the Justice of Earnings: A New Specification of the Justice Evaluation Function
Author / Institution: Guillermina Jasso / American Journal of Sociology
Description: Classic article in justice evaluation theory. Relevant for distributive justice, inequality, social comparison, and mathematical sociology in social psychology.
Tags: justice; inequality; distributive justice; social comparison; classic article
Notes: Appears in the current RC42 website content; link to publisher DOI page.

Title: Sociology & Social Psychology
Description: TRAILS resource entry and related materials for teaching sociology and social psychology. Useful as a gateway to syllabi, assignments, and classroom activities.
Tags: teaching; syllabus; assignments; sociological social psychology; TRAILS
Access type: Requires TRAILS/ASA access for some materials
Notes: Good model for building RC42 member-contributed teaching resources.

Title: TRAILS: Teaching Resources and Innovations Library for Sociology
Description: Peer-reviewed repository of sociology teaching materials, including syllabi, assignments, lectures, class activities, and assessments.
Tags: teaching; repository; pedagogy; syllabi; assignments; sociology
Notes: Useful as a reference model and source for teaching-resource standards.

Title: Sociology 150: Sociological Social Psychology
Description: Open syllabus on micro-social processes and macro-social consequences in sociological social psychology. Useful model for an undergraduate course outline.
Tags: syllabus; interaction; self and society; symbolic interactionism; undergraduate teaching
Notes: Older syllabus; still useful as a teaching example.

Title: Social Psychology: A Sociological Perspective
Description: Course outline using a sociological perspective on social psychology and microsociology. Useful for teaching identity, interaction, groups, and social structure.
Tags: syllabus; microsociology; social psychology; teaching; theory
Notes: Included as a candidate; verify active link before publishing.

Title: Social Psychology Readings
Description: Open reading list for social psychology. More psychological than sociological, but useful for interdisciplinary comparison and introductory teaching.
Tags: teaching; readings; social psychology; interdisciplinary; open courseware
Notes: Frame as complementary rather than core sociological social psychology.

Title: Introduction to Sociology 3e
Description: Open sociology textbook covering culture, socialization, groups, organizations, stratification, family, religion, and social change. Useful for introductory teaching.
Tags: open textbook; teaching; socialization; culture; inequality; introductory sociology
Notes: Introductory rather than specialized; useful for undergraduate foundations.

Title: Principles of Social Psychology
Description: Open social psychology textbook that can support interdisciplinary teaching. It should be framed as a psychology-oriented complement to sociological resources.
Tags: open textbook; social psychology; teaching; interdisciplinary
Notes: Psychology-oriented; include only if RC42 wants broad interdisciplinary teaching materials.

Title: World Values Survey
Description: Global comparative survey on values, beliefs, trust, gender, religion, poverty, tolerance, and social attitudes. Strong for comparative sociological social psychology.
Tags: values; attitudes; trust; religion; gender; comparative research; survey data
Notes: Excellent first-wave dataset resource; include citation guidance.

Title: European Social Survey Data Portal
Description: High-quality open data on attitudes, beliefs, behavior, trust, wellbeing, immigration, climate, democracy, and values across Europe.
Tags: attitudes; trust; wellbeing; immigration; democracy; survey data; Europe
Notes: Strong documentation; useful for methods teaching.

Title: International Social Survey Programme
Description: Cross-national survey modules on inequality, national identity, family, gender roles, religion, citizenship, social networks, and environment.
Tags: comparative research; inequality; national identity; gender; citizenship; survey data
Notes: Excellent for comparative teaching and research exercises.

Title: AmericasBarometer
Description: Leading comparative public opinion data source on democracy, governance, political trust, participation, and values across the Americas.
Tags: Latin America; democracy; trust; participation; public opinion; survey data
Access type: Open data / registration may be required
Notes: Important for regional diversity and Latin American research.

Title: Latinobarómetro 2024
Description: Public opinion survey covering 18 Latin American countries, including democracy, economy, institutions, values, and social attitudes.
Tags: Latin America; democracy; trust; values; public opinion; survey data
Notes: Priority resource for Latin American inclusion.

Title: Afrobarometer Data
Description: Survey data on democracy, governance, participation, collective action, identity, trust, and social capital across African countries.
Tags: Africa; democracy; governance; trust; participation; survey data
Notes: Strong contribution to regional diversity.

Title: Asian Barometer Survey
Description: Comparative survey on democracy, governance, political participation, democratic and authoritarian values, and trust in Asian societies.
Tags: Asia; democracy; authoritarian values; trust; participation; survey data
Notes: Useful for regional diversity; clarify access process.

Title: General Social Survey
Author / Institution: NORC at the University of Chicago
Description: Long-running U.S. survey of attitudes, behaviors, identities, morality, family, race relations, gender, social change, and public opinion.
Tags: United States; attitudes; race; gender; family; identity; survey data
Notes: U.S.-centered but methodologically important.

Title: survey
Description: R package for analyzing complex survey samples, including weights, stratification, clustering, replicate weights, and design-based inference.
Tags: R; survey analysis; weights; complex samples; methodology
Notes: Core tool for comparative survey analysis.

Title: lavaan
Description: Open-source package for latent variable models, CFA, SEM, path analysis, and growth curve models. Useful for measurement of identities, attitudes, values, and emotions.
Tags: R; CFA; SEM; latent variables; measurement; methodology
Notes: Useful for methodological innovation and teaching.

Title: igraph
Description: Open-source network analysis library available for R, Python, Mathematica, and C/C++. Useful for social networks, interaction, groups, diffusion, and relational analysis.
Tags: networks; R; Python; graph analysis; interaction; groups
Notes: Useful for relational and network-oriented work.

Title: quanteda
Description: Open-source R package for quantitative text analysis, corpus management, dictionaries, keywords, text-as-data workflows, and machine learning.
Tags: R; text analysis; discourse; culture; identity; digital methods
Notes: Useful for discourse, culture, identity, and digital methods.

Title: Global Dialogue
Description: ISA’s open-access online magazine for global and public sociology. Useful model for multilingual, internationally oriented short essays and interviews.
Tags: public sociology; ISA; global sociology; blog; essays; multilingual
Notes: Strongest institutional model for an RC42 blog/post section.

Title: Contexts Magazine
Description: Public-facing sociology magazine that makes social research accessible through essays, reviews, photo essays, and research-based commentary.
Tags: public sociology; magazine; essays; research communication; ASA
Notes: Good model for writing style and public-facing presentation.

Title: The Society Pages
Description: Open-access social science project with articles, blogs, podcasts, and public sociology resources. Useful as a model for accessible academic communication.
Tags: public sociology; blog; podcasts; research communication; teaching
Notes: Good inspiration for RC42 field notes and short essays.

Title: Transforming Society
Description: Research-informed blog with essays on inequality, migration, social justice, public policy, and social change. Useful model for thematic blog organization.
Tags: academic blog; inequality; migration; social justice; public sociology
Notes: Good model for brief research-based posts.

Title: Launching the new journal Emotions and Society – why now?
Author / Institution: Mary Holmes, Nathan Manning & Åsa Wettergren / Transforming Society
Description: Short editorial reflection on the relevance of emotions as a social and interdisciplinary field. Useful as an example of a field-building post.
Tags: emotions; field-building; academic blog; sociology of emotions
Notes: Useful as a model, not necessarily as a core resource.
